skill.name: postgresql-optimization
skill.description: PostgreSQL database optimization workflow for query tuning, indexing strategies, performance analysis, and production database management.
skill.summary: Guide PostgreSQL performance work across assessment, query tuning, indexing, configuration, maintenance, and monitoring.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks and normal PostgreSQL wording as potential shell execution and weak cryptography. Manual review found only workflow text, skill references, and copy-paste prompts; no executable code, prompt injection, network access, secret handling, or data exfiltration evidence was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command detections are Markdown inline code and fenced copy-paste prompts that reference other skills. They do not execute commands and provide no command injection path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are visible Markdown list items or fenced prompt examples. There is no Ruby code, shell syntax, or user-controlled execution behavior in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: PostgreSQL Design Text Misclassified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections appear to match ordinary words such as design or strategy in workflow text. No cryptographic algorithm or security-sensitive implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines discuss PostgreSQL indexing and database design, not encryption. No DES, MD5, SHA1, or other weak cryptographic implementation appears in context.

content.value_statement: Slow PostgreSQL systems are hard to improve without a structured review process. This skill guides assessment, query analysis, indexing, configuration tuning, maintenance, and monitoring.
actual_capabilities: Structures a PostgreSQL performance assessment across version, configuration, slow queries, resources, and bottlenecks.
Guides query analysis with EXPLAIN ANALYZE, scan types, join strategies, and execution time review.
Outlines indexing work for missing indexes, B-tree indexes, composite indexes, partial indexes, and index usage checks.
Provides configuration tuning steps for shared_buffers, work_mem, effective_cache_size, checkpoint settings, and autovacuum.
Covers maintenance planning for VACUUM, ANALYZE, table bloat checks, autovacuum monitoring, and statistics review.
Includes monitoring steps for dashboards, alerts, key metrics, and trend review.
limitations: It is a workflow guide, not an automated PostgreSQL profiler or benchmark tool.
It does not include executable scripts, SQL migrations, or database connection logic.
It depends on the user supplying query plans, metrics, configuration values, and workload context.
It references other skills that may not be installed in every environment.

use_cases.title: Diagnose Slow Production Queries
use_cases.description: Use the workflow to collect plans, identify bottlenecks, and prioritize safe query and index changes.

use_cases.title: Plan Index Improvements
use_cases.description: Review missing, composite, partial, and unused indexes before changing a PostgreSQL schema.

use_cases.title: Build a Monitoring Baseline
use_cases.description: Define dashboards, alerts, and key metrics for ongoing PostgreSQL performance visibility.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Performance Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this PostgreSQL optimization workflow to review my database performance. Ask me for version, workload, slow query symptoms, and available metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a Query Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this workflow to analyze my EXPLAIN ANALYZE output. Identify costly plan nodes, scan types, join issues, and practical tuning steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Index Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this workflow to design a PostgreSQL indexing strategy for these queries and tables. Include tradeoffs for composite and partial indexes.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Production Optimization Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this workflow to create a staged production optimization plan covering query changes, indexes, configuration, maintenance, monitoring, and rollback checks.

output_examples.input: A user provides a slow reporting query and asks where to start.
output_examples.output: The skill would produce a review path covering query plan collection, scan and join analysis, candidate indexes, test metrics, and deployment checks.

output_examples.input: A team wants to reduce database load during peak traffic.
output_examples.output: The skill would organize actions across configuration tuning, autovacuum review, slow query prioritization, dashboard setup, and alert thresholds.

output_examples.input: A developer is unsure whether a new composite index is needed.
output_examples.output: The skill would guide comparison of filter columns, sort order, selectivity, write overhead, existing index usage, and validation with realistic workload data.

best_practices: Collect baseline query plans and metrics before changing indexes or configuration.
Test each optimization against representative data and workload patterns.
Pair performance changes with monitoring so regressions are visible quickly.
anti_patterns: Adding indexes without checking write overhead, selectivity, and existing index coverage.
Changing global PostgreSQL settings without measuring workload-specific impact.
Treating one slow query plan as representative of all production behavior.

faq.question: Does this skill connect to my PostgreSQL database?
faq.answer: No. It is a workflow guide and does not include database connection code or credentials handling.

faq.question: Can it generate SQL changes?
faq.answer: It can guide index and query improvement discussions, but users should review and test any SQL before production use.

faq.question: What inputs work best?
faq.answer: Provide PostgreSQL version, slow query text, EXPLAIN ANALYZE output, table sizes, indexes, configuration values, and workload context.

faq.question: Is it suitable for production systems?
faq.answer: Yes, as a planning workflow. Production changes still need backups, staging tests, monitoring, and rollback plans.

faq.question: Does it replace a DBA?
faq.answer: No. It helps structure analysis and decisions, but expert review is important for high-risk database changes.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
